rulings.Case Title: BRASIL SF, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. KEVIN O'CONNOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enforce Settlement And/Or Enter Judgment Under Ccp Sec 664.6 And Request For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 16, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT KEVIN O'CONNOR'S Motion To Enforce Settlement And/Or Enter Judgment Under CCP Sec 664.6 And Request For Attorneys Fees. Denied. Defendant has not provided sufficient evidence establishing that Plaintiff is in breach of the Settlement Agreement. Defendant provides no evidence establishing that Plaintiff has been subject to tax liabilities. Further, Defendant does not specifically identify what payments were late, nor does he provide direct evidence of these alleged late payments. The Court did not consider the evidence submitted for the first time in reply, as Defendant has made no showing as to why the evidence could not have been submitted with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KARMA QUICK VS. JONATHAN FRIED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, December 16, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF KARMA QUICK'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's unopposed motion is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Form Interrogatories is GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to her request for production of documents is GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion to have requests for admissions deemed admitted is GRANTED. Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREN HAULE VS. NEW DEAL DESIGN LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 16, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT NEW DEAL DESIGN LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues. Defendant's Motion is denied. First, P has presented sufficient evidence to establish a prima facie case of sex discrimination. Defendant's assertion that Plaintiff was not a member of the protected class is unavailing, as the statute only requires that the discrimination be because of pregnancy, not during pregnancy. (See Gov. Code § 12940(a).) Additionally, triable issues of material exist as to whether Plaintiff was competently performing at the time of termination (Plaintiff's UMF #22, 71, 107) and whether Defendant exhibited discriminatory animus toward Plaintiff as a result of her pregnancy (Plaintiff's UMF #35, 37 - 39). Defendant's hearsay objection is denied. (See Evid. Code §§ 1220-27.) Plaintiff has also met her burden of establishing the existence of triable issues of material fact as to whether Defendant had a legitimate business reason for Plaintiff's termination. (See Defendant's UMF #155, 156; Plaintiff's UMF #80, 82-85, 87, 89-97.) Second, Defendant is not entitled to judgment as a matter of law for either remaining cause of action. Defendant presents evidence establishing only that CFRA and FMLA do not apply. However, Defendant's second cause of action is based on the public policy delineated in the FEHA, which the Motion fails to address. Finally, Defendant presents no evidence that Plaintiff failed to exhaust her administrative remedies with respect to Plaintiff's third cause of action, and there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the parties agreed upon a date of reinstatement. (See 2 CCR 11043(b)(1); Plaintiff's UMF # 58, 60-62.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHELSEA KHOO VS. CITY COLLEGE OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To Judicial Council Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, December 16, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Judicial Council Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories is DENIED. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $900.00. Sanctions to be paid within ten (10) days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HELENA HUOH VS. ARIEL BENTOLILA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Motion To Disqualify The Law Of Bornstein & Bornstein
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 16, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS ABEHET TEKIE, ARIEL BANTOLILA'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY THE LAW OF BORNSTEIN & BORNSTEIN. Motion granted. The subject matter of both this action and the related action (case number CUD-14-650239) is the tenancy of Defendants at the subject property. Counsel was aware that Defendants were represented in the first action, yet communicated indirectly with Defendants on numerous occasions regarding Defendants' tenancy at the subject property. This is a violation of Calif. Rule of Prof. Conduct 2-100. Further, the risk that counsel has obtained an unfair advantage and impacted the integrity of the judicial proceedings in both actions sufficiently outweighs the clients' right to counsel of choice, especially here, where neither opposition brief argues that any hardship would result to either client if the Motion is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
